title: AgentLedger Agent Outcome Graph
description: Formal contract for the Agent Outcome Graph (CLAUDE.md Phase 3): the moat that makes cost -> agent -> outcome -> value queryable with an attribution confidence on every edge. Nodes are identities (human AND non-human/agent), agent runs, and business outcomes; edges connect them and ALWAYS carry an attribution_confidence in [0,1] (1.0 for deterministic/agent-stamped links, <1.0 for probabilistic links from the attribution matcher). This document is the abstract serialization; its physical realization is the Postgres + ClickHouse tables/views described in README.md (identities/agents -> v_identities, agent_runs, outcomes, v_outcome_graph). SECURITY: nodes carry identifiers and categorical/financial attributes only — never raw prompt/completion content (CLAUDE.md rule 2).

tenant_id.description: Tenant that owns this subgraph; every node and edge is scoped to it.

confidence.description: Attribution confidence in [0,1]. 1.0 == deterministic (SDK/agent-stamped); <1.0 == probabilistic (attribution matcher, time/identity/token signals).

identity_type.description: First-class non-human identities (agents) sit alongside humans — CLAUDE.md Phase 3.

owner_user_id.description: Agents only: the human identity that owns/operates this NHI.

total_cost_usd.description: Fully-observed AI cost of the run (sum of its llm_calls).

edge_type.description: performed_by: agent_run -> identity(human). operates: identity(human) -> identity(agent NHI). incurred_cost: agent_run -> cost (deterministic). produced: agent_run -> outcome (carries the matcher's confidence). valued_at: outcome -> business value (deterministic).

deterministic.description: True when the link is asserted (agent-stamped/SDK/structural), implying attribution_confidence == 1.0.
